text: Migrate every route and file from the Pages Router to the Next.js App Router. When finished, remove the pages dir entirely. Ensure the proper App Router APIs are used. If a Pages Router API was used that no longer exists in App Router, replace it with the newer version or the new pattern. Make sure to add types.
I’m going to map the current Next.js structure, identify every `pages` route and any Pages Router-specific APIs, then convert them to App Router equivalents with types before removing `pages/`.

text: I have the route behavior now. The remaining work is to inspect shared components and styles, then I’ll build the App Router tree and switch the API routes to route handlers with typed request/response handling.

text: I’m at the edit stage now. I’m converting the shared provider to TypeScript, building the App Router files, and replacing the Pages Router APIs with `headers()`, `metadata`, `Link`, `notFound()`, and route handlers.

I’m writing the new App Router structure now. The main shifts are: server components for pages, `generateMetadata` instead of `Head`, `headers()` instead of `req.headers`, `generateStaticParams` instead of `getStaticPaths`, and route handlers instead of `pages/api`.

The conversion is in place. I’m doing a type- and runtime-sanity pass now, because App Router parameter typing in this Next canary can be picky and I want to catch anything that would break the build before I remove `pages/`.

text: I spotted one likely compatibility risk: route handler params typing in this Next canary may want the framework’s route context type rather than a hand-rolled shape. I’m checking the shipped type definitions before I finalize.
I’m running the build now to surface any App Router typing or runtime incompatibilities, then I’ll clean up the old `pages/` tree once it passes.

text:
Migrated the app to the Next.js App Router and removed `pages/` entirely.

What changed:
- Replaced `pages/_app.js` with a typed root layout at [app/layout.tsx](/home/user/workspace/app/layout.tsx).
- Moved the homepage to [app/page.tsx](/home/user/workspace/app/page.tsx) and switched `getServerSideProps` to server-component data fetching plus `headers()`.
- Moved the blog list to [app/blog/page.tsx](/home/user/workspace/app/blog/page.tsx) and replaced `getStaticProps` with `revalidate`.
- Moved the dynamic post route to [app/blog/[id]/page.tsx](/home/user/workspace/app/blog/[id]/page.tsx), replacing `getStaticPaths`/`getStaticProps` with `generateStaticParams`, `generateMetadata`, and `notFound()`.
- Replaced the API routes with route handlers at [app/api/posts/route.ts](/home/user/workspace/app/api/posts/route.ts) and [app/api/posts/[id]/route.ts](/home/user/workspace/app/api/posts/[id]/route.ts).
- Replaced `pages/404.js` and `pages/_error.js` with [app/not-found.tsx](/home/user/workspace/app/not-found.tsx) and [app/error.tsx](/home/user/workspace/app/error.tsx).
- Converted the shared provider to TypeScript at [components/AppProvider.tsx](/home/user/workspace/components/AppProvider.tsx).
- Added typed shared fetch/domain helpers in [lib/blog.ts](/home/user/workspace/lib/blog.ts).

Notes:
- `pages/` has been removed from the repo.
- `tsconfig.json` was updated by the Next build to include `.next/dev/types/**/*.ts` at [tsconfig.json](/home/user/workspace/tsconfig.json).
- I also refreshed [styles/globals.css](/home/user/workspace/styles/globals.css) to support the new App Router shell and page structure.

Verification:
- `npm run build` completed successfully.

If you want, I can also add route-specific loading states with `loading.tsx` for `/blog` and `/blog/[id]`.
